content: Image is a read-only template with instructions for creating a container. A container is a runnable instance of an image. You can create multiple containers from the same image.
Docker supports three volume types: named volumes (managed by Docker), bind mounts (host path mapped), and tmpfs (in-memory only). Named volumes are preferred for production data persistence.
Docker provides bridge (default, isolated), host (shares host network), and overlay (multi-host swarm) network drivers. Containers on the same bridge network can communicate by container name.

COPY simply copies files from host to container. ADD has extra features: auto-extracting tar archives and fetching URLs. Best practice: always use COPY unless you specifically need ADD's extra features.

HEALTHCHECK instruction tells Docker how to test a container's health. Example: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1. Compose can use depends_on with condition: service_healthy.
